| [SEC-018] | рџџҰ Medium | JS `XmlParser.parseXmlManual` root-match regex `/^<([a-zA-Z_][\w.-]*)[^>]*>([\s\S]*)<\/\1\s*>$/s` had O(n Г— \|tagName\|) backtracking on adversarial single-element documents; SEC-016 fixed `parseXmlChildren` but did not replace root detection | Replaced `rootMatch` regex + `selfClose` regex with new private `extractRootContent()` method: O(1) backward scan from the final `>` char to locate the closing tag by tag-name string comparison; `extractRootContent` also subsumes the self-closing detection; all 77 parser tests pass; no backreference regexes remain in the Node.js parse path | 2026-04-06 |
| [SEC-017] | рџџ  High | JS `XmlParser` constructor assigned `maxElements` without validation; `NaN`/`Infinity`/`0`/`-1` silently disabled the element-bomb guard (`elementCount > NaN` is always `false`) вЂ” a consumer passing `SecurityParser({ maxKeys: NaN })` propagated `NaN` through `getMaxKeys()` в†’ `XmlParser`, killing the Node.js-path guard | Constructor now clamps via `Number.isFinite(maxElements) && maxElements >= 1 ? maxElements : 10_000`; 7 new tests added for NaN/Infinity/0/-1/valid cases in `xml-parser.test.ts`; all 77 parser tests pass | 2026-04-06 |
| [SEC-019] | рџ”µ Low | `SecurityParser` constructor `maxKeys` docblock did not document its dual role as XML element-count cap; `XmlParser` constructor `maxElements` docblock did not document NaN/Infinity clamping | Expanded `@param options.maxKeys` JSDoc to note `XmlParser` element-cap coupling and security implication; expanded `@param maxElements` to note clamping contract | 2026-04-06 |
| [SEC-020] | рџџ  High | `SecurityParser` and `SecurityGuard` JS constructors accepted `NaN`/`Infinity` for all numeric options; `x > NaN` is always `false`, silently disabling all 4 `assertMax*` guards simultaneously; `SecurityGuard(NaN).assertSafeKeys` could recurse without depth termination (stack overflow risk on adversarial deep YAML/JSON); PHP `int` type precludes this class of bug | `SecurityParser` constructor uses new private `clampOption()` helper: rejects `undefined` and non-finite values (NaN, Infinity), falls back to defaults; `SecurityGuard` constructor clamps `maxDepth` via `Number.isFinite()`; `0` and negative values preserved (they tighten guards, not loosen); 9 new `SecurityParser` clamping tests + 3 `SecurityGuard` clamping tests; 707 total tests pass | 2026-04-06 |
| [SEC-016] | рџ”µ Low | JS `XmlParser` `parseXmlChildren` used `[\s\S]*?` with backreference `\1` (potential O(nВІ) behaviour under adversarial input) | Replaced regex-based `parseXmlChildren` with an `indexOf`-based linear scanner using a nesting counter to locate matching closing tags; O(n) guaranteed; added private `addChild()` helper; updated constructor docblock and `maxElements` guard comment; 675 tests pass | 2026-04-06 |
| [BUILD-003] | рџ”µ Low | `bench` script in `packages/js/package.json` echoed placeholder while `benchmarks/get.bench.ts` and `benchmarks/parse.bench.ts` existed but were not wired | Updated bench script to `npx vitest bench benchmarks/` вЂ” wires both existing bench files to `pnpm bench` | 2026-04-06 |
| [TEST-034] | рџџ  Moderate | No end-to-end JS test verifying `XmlAccessor` with custom `SecurityParser({ maxKeys: N })` rejects XML with >N opening tags | Added 2 integration tests to `packages/js/tests/accessors/accessors.test.ts` (XmlAccessor block): one asserts `SecurityException` when opening-tag count exceeds `maxKeys`, one asserts no throw when within limit | 2026-04-06 |

 yaml-parser.ts MSI improved from 74.56% в†’ 88.83% via code restructuring and targeted test additions. Key changes: (1) `assertNoUnsafeConstructs` for-of entries() conversion; (2) 4 `?? ''` в†’ `as string` type casts; (3) ternary return refactored to `if (Array.isArray(result))` guard pattern; (4) 3 targeted tests killing endsWith/arithmetic surviving mutations. Investigation finding: `coverageAnalysis: 'all'` in Stryker with vitest instruments the code with a runtime mutant switcher; mutations reported as "Survived" may be genuinely equivalent even when manual application causes test failures вЂ” always check the instrumented code in `.stryker-tmp/` to confirm the exact mutated expression before concluding killability. Root cause of apparent LogicalOperator false-survival at yaml-parser.ts line 27: Stryker mutates `(A && B && C)` as `(A || B) && C`, not `A || B && C` вЂ” operator precedence in the instrumented wrapper groups differently from the literal string substitution shown in the report.
